notes: NI Analog Dreams — hybrid analog/digital synthesizer instrument in Kontakt. Built from samples of vintage analog hardware (Jupiter-8, Oberheim, Prophet-5, ARP 2600 etc.) combined with Kontakt's internal synthesis engine. Each preset uses 8 macro Quick Controls — the labels change per preset but the CC numbers (14–21) are always the same. The QC descriptions below are the most common default assignments; some presets remap them for patch-specific controls. Rule of thumb: QC1 is almost always filter-related, QC8 is almost always reverb/space or effects. For exact labels on any preset: open the instrument in Kontakt and read the QC knob labels at the top of the panel. Supports velocity and aftertouch on many patches.

Modulation.description: Modulation wheel — LFO depth, filter sweep, or vibrato depth depending on the preset

Soft Pedal.description: Soft pedal — some patches map to suboscillator or effects level

Breath.description: Breath — alternative modulation/dynamics source on some patches

Quick Control 1.description: Filter Cutoff — low-pass filter frequency (most common QC1 assignment across Analog Dreams presets). Sweep for classic synth filter effects.

Quick Control 2.description: Filter Resonance — filter Q / resonance peak. Combine with QC1 for self-oscillation effects on high-resonance presets.

Quick Control 3.description: Attack — amplitude or filter envelope attack time. Pads: slow for long fade-in. Leads/Bass: fast for punchy immediate entry.

Quick Control 4.description: Release — amplitude envelope release time. Short for tight, rhythmic patches; long for evolving pads and swells.

Quick Control 5.description: LFO Rate — low-frequency oscillator speed for modulation (tremolo, vibrato, filter wobble depending on preset routing)

Quick Control 6.description: LFO Depth / Mod Amount — how much the LFO affects its target. At high values can produce dramatic filter sweeps or pitch vibrato.

Quick Control 7.description: FX Mix / Character — effects wet/dry blend or patch-specific character control (distortion, chorus depth, bit-crush etc. — check preset QC label)

Quick Control 8.description: Reverb / Space — reverb or delay mix. On most Analog Dreams presets this controls the ambient tail size — essential for the signature wide pad sounds.

Attack.description: Global envelope attack (maps alongside QC3 on most patches)

Release.description: Global envelope release (maps alongside QC4 on most patches)

Glide.description: Portamento/glide time — many Analog Dreams leads and bass patches respond to this
